title: [CI:DOCS] push/pull docs: clarify supported transports
body:
The man pages of podman push/pull were incomplete in explaining all
supported transports.  To keep things simple, explain the defaults,
refer to containers-transports(5) and give some examples.

Fixes: #10730
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@mtrmac @rhatdan PTAL

Note to self: once merged, open a PR against Buildah.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

What about `podman save`?  Its documentation says:
```
     **podman  save**  saves  an image to either docker-archive, oci-archive, oci-dir (directory with oci manifest type), or docker-dir (directory with v2s2 manifest type) on the local machine, default is docker-archive.
…
 OPTIONS
    --format=format
       Save image to oci-archive, oci-dir (directory with oci manifest type), or docker-dir (directory with v2s2 manifest type)

             --format oci-archive
             --format oci-dir
             --format docker-dir
```

Does `podman save` use `oci-dir` instead of `oci` on purpose? Is docker-archive skipped from format on purpose?

> What about `podman save`? Its documentation says:
> [...]
> Does `podman save` use `oci-dir` instead of `oci` on purpose?

Yes. They are two different things. `oci-dir` is not using the `oci` transport but the `dir` transport; the image is stored as an OCI image.  With `docker-dir` the image is stored as a Docker image.

> Is docker-archive skipped from format on purpose?

I'll open another PR to fix that.  Thanks for highlighting!
